parsed_review.summary: CP00-390 is a clean descriptor-only closeout pack for the RP12.P09 permission fixture slice (10 RP12 units, RP12.P09.M05.S21-RP12.P09.M06.S08, risk A). Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack-level claim is supported: production_ready is correctly false (descriptor verified, pending review + human approval), and no premature enterprise-trust claim is made. Implementation (registry.js/service.js/validators.js) builds only frozen descriptor data structures with descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, raw_payload_included:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true on every row; freezeCp390Result stamps a comprehensive no-write/no-dispatch/no-leak attestation (no billing/proforma/invoice/write-down runtime, no DB rows, no object storage, no audit/state writes, no Hermes runtime receipt). No I/O, fetch, fs, child_process, env reads, conflict markers, or debug code in changed source. All 7 required public exports exist and are re-exported via index.js wildcards; 3 dedicated tests cover coverage, descriptor-only rows, and authority boundaries. The contract (current_pack, cp390_requirements, cp390_no_write_attestation, p09_permission_fixture_slice_descriptor) is deep-equal-validated against source by the validator, preventing drift. Plan, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, and next-pack-queue agree (deviation_from_plan:false; CP00-390 is the active pack after CP00-389). Authority boundary holds: Claude is a read-only reviewer (Read/Grep/Glob), not final approver (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false) enforced in code, attestation, contract, and tests. Hermes patterns are used only for the deterministic evidence packet (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime. Review hardening: auth preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON, 1436B stdout), validation-report shows allowed tools only and no forbidden tools, and no pre-existing malformed/auth-failed/0-byte/fenced/tool-call-shaped review evidence is being passed off as valid; post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent at this stage. command-evidence reports all implementation/plan/repo-wide validation commands at exit 0. One non-blocking P3: changed-file-scope.json classifies the pack's own evidence dir (and new-session-handoff.md) under both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. No P0/P1/P2 issues; pack and goal closeout are not blocked.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies pack evidence dir as both implementation and unrelated
findings.details: The pack's own evidence directory 'docs/closeout-packs/cp00-390/' and 'docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md' appear in BOTH untracked_implementation_files (lines 131-132) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 137-138). These classifications are mutually exclusive by intent: a file is either part of this pack's implementation/evidence or an unrelated preserved dirty file. The pack evidence dir is genuinely part of CP00-390 (it is referenced in manifest.required_pack_artifacts), so listing it under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved is the incorrect entry. The defect is isolated to this generated review-tooling artifact; the manifest's own worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved correctly excludes the pack dir and handoff doc. It has no effect on any validation gate, the descriptor-only/no-write boundaries, or the production_ready status, and does not cause unrelated dirty files to be pulled into the pack.
findings.recommendation: In the changed-file-scope generator, ensure each worktree path is assigned to exactly one bucket: keep 'docs/closeout-packs/cp00-390/' and 'docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md' in untracked_implementation_files and remove them from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (matching the manifest classification). Non-blocking; can be corrected in a future tooling pass.
findings.post_review_disposition: non_blocking_informational; does not block CP00-390 pack or goal closeout; recommend correcting changed-file-scope.json bucket assignment in a subsequent review-tooling update.
